title: Callista palpebrae
intro: Callista palpebrae, also known as Callistemon palpebrae or Small Bottlebrush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Callista palpebrae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic activities. Herbal preparations made from Callista palpebrae, such as infusions and tinctures, are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat coughs, colds, and skin irritations, as well as to support wound healing.
taxonomy: Callista palpebrae belongs to the phylum Mollusca and specifically to the class Gastropoda. Within the Gastropoda class, it falls under the subclass Caenogastropoda. Further classification places it in the family Cerastidae, which is a group of marine gastropod mollusks. The genus Callista is a group of saltwater clams, and Callista palpebrae is a species within this genus. This classification reflects its characteristics as a type of marine bivalve mollusk.
